volumeTitle: Trojan Women. Iphigenia among the Taurians. Ion
label: Ion (Section 747 - Section 880)

sections.label: Section 747
sections.original: γυναῖκες , ἱστῶν τῶν ἐμῶν καὶ κερκίδος
sections.translation: Maidens mine , my trusty servants at the loom and web , declare to me how my lord hath fared as touching the question of offspring which brought us hither :

sections.label: Section 750
sections.original: σημήνατʼ · εἰ γὰρ ἀγαθά μοι μηνύσετε ,
sections.translation: for if ye give me good news , ye will cause joy to a mistress who will not prove faithless to her word .

sections.label: Section 752
sections.original: ἰὼ δαῖμον .
sections.translation: O fortune !

sections.label: Section 753
sections.original: τὸ φροίμιον μὲν τῶν λόγων οὐκ εὐτυχές .
sections.translation: This prelude to your speech is unlucky .

sections.label: Section 754
sections.original: ἰὼ τλᾶμον .
sections.translation: Woe is me !

sections.label: Section 755
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἦ τι θεσφάτοισι δεσποτῶν νοσῶ ;
sections.translation: Can it be that the oracles delivered to my master wound me at all ?

sections.label: Section 756
sections.original: εἶἑν · τί δρῶμεν ; θάνατος ὧν κεῖται πέρι ⋯
sections.translation: Enough ! why have aught to do with that which brings down death ?

sections.label: Section 757
sections.original: τίς ἥδε μοῦσα , χὡ φόβος τίνων πέρι ;
sections.translation: What means this piteous strain ? wherefore this alarm ?

sections.label: Section 758
sections.original: εἴπωμεν ἢ σιγῶμεν ; ἢ τί δράσομεν ;
sections.translation: Are we to speak or keep silence ? What shall we do ?

sections.label: Section 759
sections.original: εἴφʼ · ὡς ἔχεις γε συμφοράν τινʼ εἰς ἐμέ .
sections.translation: Speak ; for thou hast somewhat to tell that touches me .

sections.label: Section 760
sections.original: εἰρήσεταί τοι , κεἰ θανεῖν μέλλω διπλῇ .
sections.translation: Then speak I will , though twice to die were mine . O mistress mine ! never shalt thou hold a babe within thy arms or clasp him to thy breast .

sections.label: Section 762a
sections.original: ὤμοι , θάνοιμι .
sections.translation: Ah me ! would I were dead !

sections.label: Section 763
sections.original: θύγατερ .
sections.translation: My daughter !

sections.label: Section 763b
sections.original: ὦ τάλαινʼ
sections.translation: O woe is me for my calamity ! Mine is a heritage of suffering and woe that poisons life , good friends .

sections.label: Section 765
sections.original: τέκνον .
sections.translation: Ah , my child , ’ tis death to us !

sections.label: Section 765b
sections.original: αἰαῖ αἰαῖ ·
sections.translation: Ah me ! ah me ! grief drives its weapon through this heart of mine .

sections.label: Section 769
sections.original: μήπω στενάξῃς ⋯
sections.translation: Stay thy lamentations .

sections.label: Section 769b
sections.original: ἀλλὰ πάρεισι γόοι .
sections.translation: Nay , but sorrow lodges here .

sections.label: Section 770
sections.original: πρὶν ἂν μάθωμεν ⋯
sections.translation: Till we learn —

sections.label: Section 770b
sections.original: ἀγγελίαν τίνα μοι ;
sections.translation: Ah , what further news is there for me ?

sections.label: Section 771
sections.original: εἰ ταὐτὰ πράσσων δεσπότης τῆς συμφορᾶς
sections.translation: Whether our master is in the same plight and shares thy misfortune , or thou art alone in thy misery .

sections.label: Section 774
sections.original: κείνῳ μέν , ὦ γεραιέ , παῖδα Λοξίας
sections.translation: On him , old sir , Loxias hath bestowed a son ,

sections.label: Section 775
sections.original: ἔδωκεν , ἰδίᾳ δʼ εὐτυχεῖ ταύτης δίχα .
sections.translation: and he is enjoying his good fortune apart from her .

sections.label: Section 776
sections.original: τόδʼ ἐπὶ τῷδε κακὸν ἄκρον ἔλακες
sections.translation: Herein hast thou declared a further evil crowning all , a grief for me to mourn .

sections.label: Section 778
sections.original: πότερα δὲ φῦναι δεῖ γυναικὸς ἔκ τινος
sections.translation: The child of whom thou speakest — is he some woman ’ s destined babe , or did the god declare the fate of one already born ?

sections.label: Section 780
sections.original: ἤδη πεφυκότʼ ἐκτελῆ νεανίαν
sections.translation: A youth already born and grown to man ’ s estate doth Phoebus give to him ; for I was there myself .

sections.label: Section 782
sections.original: πῶς φῄς ; ἄφατον ἄφατον ἀναύδητον
sections.translation: What sayest thou ? nor tongue nor lip should speak the word thou tellest me .

sections.label: Section 785
sections.original: κἄμοιγε . πῶς δʼ ὁ χρησμὸς ἐκπεραίνεται ,
sections.translation: And me . But declare more clearly how this oracle is finding its fulfilment , and say who is the child .

sections.label: Section 787
sections.original: ὅτῳ ξυναντήσειεν ἐκ θεοῦ συθεὶς
sections.translation: Whomso thy husband first should meet as he issued from the shrine , him the god gave him for his son .

sections.label: Section 789
sections.original: ὀττοτοττοτοῖ · τὸν δʼ ἐμὸν ἄτεκνον ἄτεκνον ἔλακεν
sections.translation: Ah me ! my fate , it seems , has doomed me to a childless

sections.label: Section 790
sections.original: ἄρα βίοτον ; ἐρημίᾳ δʼ ὀρφανοὺς
sections.translation: life , and all forlorn am I to dwell in ray halls , without an heir .

sections.label: Section 792
sections.original: τίς οὖν ἐχρήσθη ; τῷ συνῆψʼ ἴχνος ποδὸς
sections.translation: To whom did the oracle refer ? whom did our poor lady ’ s husband meet ? how and where did he see him ?

sections.label: Section 794
sections.original: οἶσθʼ , ὦ φίλη δέσποινα , τὸν νεανίαν
sections.translation: Dear mistress mine , dost know that youth

sections.label: Section 795
sections.original: ὃς τόνδʼ ἔσαιρε ναόν ; οὗτος ἔσθʼ ὁ παῖς .
sections.translation: that was sweeping yonder shrine ? He is that son .

sections.label: Section 796
sections.original: ἀνʼ ὑγρὸν ἀμπταίην αἰθέρα πόρσω γαί-
sections.translation: Oh ! for wings to cleave the liquid air beyond the land of Hellas , away to the western stars , so keen the anguish of my soul , my friends !

sections.label: Section 800
sections.original: ὄνομα δὲ ποῖον αὐτὸν ὀνομάζει πατήρ ;
sections.translation: Dost know the name his father gave to him , or is that left as yet unsettled and unsaid ?

sections.label: Section 802
sections.original: Ἴωνʼ , ἐπείπερ πρῶτος ἤντησεν πατρί .
sections.translation: He called him Ion , because he was the first to cross his path .

sections.label: Section 803
sections.original: μητρὸς δʼ ὁποίας ἐστὶν —
sections.translation: Who is his mother ?

sections.label: Section 803b
sections.original: οὐκ ἔχω φράσαι .
sections.translation: That I cannot say . But ,— to tell thee all I know , old sir ,—

sections.label: Section 805
sections.original: παιδὸς προθύσων ξένια καὶ γενέθλια
sections.translation: her lord is gone , with furtive step , into the hallowed tent , there to offer on this child ’ s behalf such gifts and victims as are offered for a birth , and with his new-found son to celebrate the feast .

sections.label: Section 808
sections.original: δέσποινα , προδεδόμεσθα — σὺν γάρ σοι νοσῶ —
sections.translation: Mistress mine , we are betrayed by thy husband , fellow-sufferers thou and I ; ’ tis a deep-laid plot to outrage us

sections.label: Section 810
sections.original: ὑβριζόμεσθα δωμάτων τʼ Ἐρεχθέως
sections.translation: and drive us from Erechtheus ’ halls . And this I say not from any hatred of thy lord but because I bear thee more love than him ; for he , after coming as a stranger to thy city and thy home , and wedding thee , and of thy heritage taking full possession ,

sections.label: Section 815
sections.original: ἄλλης γυναικὸς παῖδας ἐκκαρπούμενος
sections.translation: has been detected in a secret marriage with another woman , by whom he hath children . His secret will I now disclose ; when he found thee barren , he was not content to share with thee thy hard lot , but took to himself a slave to be his stealthy paramour

sections.label: Section 820
sections.original: τὸν παῖδʼ ἔφυσεν , ἐξενωμένον δέ τῳ
sections.translation: and thus begat a son , whom he sent abroad , giving him to some Delphian maid to nurse ; and , to escape detection , the child was dedicated to the god and reared in his temple . But when he heard his boy was grown to manhood , he persuaded thee to come hither to inquire about thy childless state .

sections.label: Section 825
sections.original: κᾆθʼ ὁ θεὸς οὐκ ἐψεύσαθʼ , ὅδε δʼ ἐψεύσατο
sections.translation: And after this , ’ twas not the god that lied , but thy husband , who long had been rearing the child , and he it was that wove this tissue of falsehood , intending , if he were detected , to refer it to the god , whereas if he escaped exposure , to repel all odium , he meant to vest the sovereignty in this son of his .

sections.label: Section 830
sections.original: καινὸν δὲ τοὔνομʼ ἀνὰ χρόνον πεπλασμένον
sections.translation: Likewise he devised anew his name , coined to suit the circumstances , Ion , because , as he asserts , he met him on his way .

sections.label: Section 832
sections.original: οἴμοι , κακούργους ἄνδρας ὡς αἰεὶ στυγῶ ,
sections.translation: Ah ! how I ever hate the wicked

sections.label: Section 835
sections.original: θέλοιμι μᾶλλον ἢ κακὸν σοφώτερον .
sections.translation: who plot unrighteousness and then cunningly trick it out . Far rather would I have a virtuous friend of no great intellect than a knave of subtler wit .

sections.label: Section 836
sections.original: καὶ τῶνδʼ ἁπάντων ἔσχατον πείσῃ κακόν ·
sections.translation: Of all thy wretched fate this will be the crowning sorrow , the bringing to thy house to be its lord some slave-girl ’ s child , whose mother is unknown , himself of no account . For this evil had been to itself confined ,

sections.label: Section 840
sections.original: μητρός , πιθών σε , σὴν λέγων ἀπαιδίαν ,
sections.translation: had he persuaded thee , pleading thy childlessness , to let him establish in the house some high-born mother ’ s son ; or if this had displeased thee , he ought to have sought a daughter of Aeolus in marriage . Wherefore must thou now put thy woman ’ s wit to work ; either take the dagger , or by guile

sections.label: Section 845
sections.original: ἢ φαρμάκοισι σὸν κατακτεῖναι πόσιν
sections.translation: or poison slay thy husband and his son , ere they deal out death to thee ; since if thou spare him , thou wilt lose thy own life ; for when two foes meet beneath one roof , one or the other must rue it .

sections.label: Section 850
sections.original: ἐγὼ μὲν οὖν σοι καὶ συνεκπονεῖν θέλω ,
sections.translation: Myself too am ready to share this labour with thee , and to help destroy the child when I have made my way into the chamber where he is furnishing the feast , and so repaying my masters for my maintenance I am willing either to die or still behold the light of life . ’ Tis but a single thing that brands the slave with shame —

sections.label: Section 855
sections.original: τοὔνομα · τὰ δʼ ἄλλα πάντα τῶν ἐλευθέρων
sections.translation: his name ; in all else no upright slave is a whit worse than freeborn men .

sections.label: Section 857
sections.original: κἀγώ , φίλη δέσποινα , συμφορὰν θέλω
sections.translation: I too , beloved mistress , am ready to share thy fate , be it death or victory .

sections.label: Section 859
sections.original: ὦ ψυχά , πῶς σιγάσω ;
sections.translation: Ah ! my suffering soul ! how am I to keep silence ?

sections.label: Section 860
sections.original: πῶς δὲ σκοτίας ἀναφήνω
sections.translation: Am I to disclose the secrets of my love and lose all claim to modesty ? What is there to keep me back any longer ? With whom have I to pit myself in virtue ’ s lists ? Hath not my husband proved untrue ?

sections.label: Section 865
sections.original: στέρομαι δʼ οἴκων , στέρομαι παίδων ,
sections.translation: Home and children , both are torn from me ; all hope is dead ; I have not realized my wish to set the matter straight , by hushing up my former union and saying naught about my son of sorrow .

sections.label: Section 870
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐ τὸ Διὸς πολύαστρον ἕδος
sections.translation: No ! by the starry seat of Zeus , by her whose home is on my rocks , and by the hallowed strand of Triton ’ s mere with brimming flood , I will no more conceal my love ; for if

sections.label: Section 875
sections.original: ἀπονησαμένη ῥᾴων ἔσομαι .
sections.translation: I can lift that burden from my breast I shall rest easier . With tears my eyes are streaming and my heart is wrung with anguish for the treacherous counsels both of men and gods ,— traitors they ! as I will show ,

sections.label: Section 880
sections.original: λέκτρων προδότας ἀχαρίστους .
sections.translation: ungrateful traitors to their loves !
